practiceMcqs.question: Which article of the Indian Constitution outlines the power of Parliament to amend the Constitution and the procedure therefor?
practiceMcqs.explanation: Article 368 in Part XX of the Indian Constitution explicitly provides the power of Parliament to amend the Constitution and prescribes the specific procedures and majorities required.

practiceMcqs.question: From which country's constitution was the procedure for amending the Indian Constitution borrowed?
practiceMcqs.explanation: The procedure for amending the Indian Constitution was adopted from the Constitution of South Africa (Article 368).

practiceMcqs.question: Which of the following constitutional amendments is widely referred to as the 'Mini-Constitution' of India?
practiceMcqs.explanation: The 42nd Constitutional Amendment Act, 1976, enacted during the Internal Emergency, made sweeping changes across the Constitution, earning it the moniker 'Mini-Constitution'.

practiceMcqs.question: Which amendment act converted the Right to Property from a Fundamental Right into a legal right under Article 300A?
practiceMcqs.explanation: The 44th Constitutional Amendment Act, 1978, removed the Right to Property from Part III (Article 31) and placed it as a legal right under Article 300A in Part XII.

practiceMcqs.question: In which landmark Supreme Court case was the 'Basic Structure Doctrine' propounded?
practiceMcqs.explanation: The 13-judge constitutional bench in the Kesavananda Bharati v. State of Kerala case (1973) formulated the Basic Structure Doctrine, placing an implied limitation on Parliament's amending powers.

practiceMcqs.question: Emergency provisions in the Indian Constitution are contained in which Part?
practiceMcqs.explanation: Part XVIII of the Constitution, comprising Articles 352 to 360, contains the comprehensive emergency provisions borrowed from the Weimar Constitution of Germany.

practiceMcqs.question: What is the parliamentary approval timeline and majority required for a National Emergency proclamation under Article 352?
options: Within 1 month by a Special Majority
Within 2 months by a Simple Majority
Within 1 month by a Simple Majority
Within 6 months by a Special Majority
practiceMcqs.correctAnswer: Within 1 month by a Special Majority
practiceMcqs.explanation: A National Emergency proclamation must be approved by both Houses of Parliament within 1 month from its date of issue by a special majority.

practiceMcqs.question: Which of the following terms was substituted for 'internal disturbance' under Article 352 by the 44th Amendment Act?
practiceMcqs.explanation: To prevent arbitrary invocation, the 44th Amendment Act (1978) replaced the vague term 'internal disturbance' with 'armed rebellion' as one of the grounds for proclaiming a National Emergency.

practiceMcqs.question: Which fundamental articles can NEVER be suspended during any emergency circumstance under post-44th Amendment provisions?
practiceMcqs.explanation: Articles 20 (protection in respect of conviction for offenses) and 21 (protection of life and personal liberty) can never be suspended under any emergency, as safeguarded by the 44th Amendment.

practiceMcqs.question: How many times has a Financial Emergency under Article 360 been proclaimed in India to date?
practiceMcqs.explanation: A Financial Emergency under Article 360 has never been proclaimed in India's constitutional history.

practiceMcqs.question: Which amendment act introduced the Tenth Schedule to the Constitution, famously known as the 'Anti-Defection Law'?
practiceMcqs.explanation: The 52nd Constitutional Amendment Act, 1985, added the Tenth Schedule to curb political defection by legislators.

practiceMcqs.question: Which amendment act lowered the voting age for Lok Sabha and Legislative Assembly elections from 21 years to 18 years?
practiceMcqs.explanation: The 61st Constitutional Amendment Act, 1988, amended Article 326 to lower the minimum voting age from 21 to 18 years.

practiceMcqs.question: What is the maximum duration for which President's Rule (Article 356) can be extended with periodic parliamentary approvals every six months?
practiceMcqs.explanation: President's Rule can be extended by 6 months at a time up to a maximum strict limit of 3 years with parliamentary approval.

practiceMcqs.question: Which constitutional amendment added Part IX and the Eleventh Schedule to give constitutional status to Panchayati Raj Institutions?
practiceMcqs.explanation: The 73rd Amendment Act, 1992, added Part IX and the Eleventh Schedule containing 29 matters for Panchayats.

practiceMcqs.question: Which article deals with the automatic suspension of provisions of Article 19 during an external emergency?
practiceMcqs.explanation: Article 358 provides for the automatic suspension of Article 19 rights as soon as a National Emergency is declared on grounds of war or external aggression.

practiceMcqs.question: Which amendment act made elementary education a Fundamental Right under Article 21A?
practiceMcqs.explanation: The 86th Constitutional Amendment Act, 2002, inserted Article 21A, making education a fundamental right for children aged 6 to 14 years.

practiceMcqs.question: What is the maximum limit placed on the size of the Council of Ministers at the Centre and States by the 91st Amendment Act (2003)?
practiceMcqs.explanation: The 91st Amendment Act capped the total number of ministers, including the Prime Minister/Chief Minister, at 15% of the total strength of the Lok Sabha or Legislative Assembly.

practiceMcqs.question: Which article establishes the GST Council, introduced by the 101st Constitutional Amendment Act?
practiceMcqs.explanation: Article 279A, added by the 101st Amendment Act (2016), establishes the GST Council chaired by the Union Finance Minister.

practiceMcqs.question: Which constitutional amendment granted constitutional status to the National Commission for Backward Classes (NCBC)?
practiceMcqs.explanation: The 102nd Amendment Act (2018) inserted Article 338B, granting constitutional body status to the NCBC.

practiceMcqs.question: Which amendment act introduced 10% reservation in educational institutions and public employment for Economically Weaker Sections (EWS)?
practiceMcqs.explanation: The 103rd Amendment Act (2019) added clauses (6) to Articles 15 and 16 to provide a 10% EWS quota.

practiceMcqs.question: What was the primary effect of the 104th Constitutional Amendment Act (2019)?
options: Extended SC/ST reservation deadlines to 2030 and discontinued Anglo-Indian nomination seats
Restored state powers over OBC lists
practiceMcqs.correctAnswer: Extended SC/ST reservation deadlines to 2030 and discontinued Anglo-Indian nomination seats
practiceMcqs.explanation: The 104th Amendment extended reservations for SCs and STs in Lok Sabha and assemblies until 2030 while ending reserved nomination seats for Anglo-Indians.

practiceMcqs.question: Which article states that if any state fails to comply with Union directions, it shall be lawful for the President to hold that constitutional machinery has failed (Article 356)?
practiceMcqs.explanation: Article 365 specifies that non-compliance with Union directions by a state gives the Centre grounds to invoke President's Rule under Article 356.

practiceMcqs.question: Which type of majority is required in each House of Parliament to pass a constitutional amendment bill under Article 368?
practiceMcqs.explanation: Article 368 requires a special majority: a majority of the total membership of each House (>50%) AND a majority of two-thirds of the members present and voting.

practiceMcqs.question: Can a joint sitting of Parliament be summoned to resolve a deadlock over a Constitutional Amendment Bill?
options: No, joint sittings are not applicable to Constitutional Amendment Bills
practiceMcqs.correctAnswer: No, joint sittings are not applicable to Constitutional Amendment Bills
practiceMcqs.explanation: Constitutional Amendment Bills must be passed separately by each House of Parliament; joint sittings are not permitted under Article 368.

practiceMcqs.question: Which amendment act nullified the Supreme Court's Golaknath ruling and affirmed Parliament's absolute power to amend Fundamental Rights?
practiceMcqs.explanation: The 24th Amendment Act (1971) amended Articles 13 and 368 to affirm that Parliament can amend any part of the Constitution, including Fundamental Rights.

practiceMcqs.question: Which article grants constitutional immunity to the President and Governors from criminal proceedings and arrest during their term of office?
practiceMcqs.explanation: Article 361 protects the President and Governors from criminal proceedings, arrest, or imprisonment while in office.

practiceMcqs.question: What is the approval timeline and majority required for proclaiming President's Rule under Article 356?
options: Within 1 month by a Special Majority
Within 2 months by a Simple Majority
Within 1 month by a Simple Majority
Within 2 months by a Special Majority
practiceMcqs.correctAnswer: Within 2 months by a Simple Majority
practiceMcqs.explanation: A proclamation of President's Rule must be approved by both Houses of Parliament within 2 months by a simple majority.

practiceMcqs.question: Which amendment act restored the explicit power of state governments to maintain their own state lists of Socially and Educationally Backward Classes (SEBCs)?
practiceMcqs.explanation: The 105th Amendment Act (2021) amended Article 342A to explicitly restore state authority to maintain individual state SEBC lists.

practiceMcqs.question: Which of the following provisions requires ratification by at least half of the state legislatures along with a special majority in Parliament?
options: Creation of new states under Article 3
Abolition of Legislative Councils under Article 169
Election of the President and its manner under Article 54 & 55
Creation of Fundamental Duties under Article 51A
practiceMcqs.correctAnswer: Election of the President and its manner under Article 54 & 55
practiceMcqs.explanation: Provisions affecting federal structures, such as the election of the President, require state ratification by at least half of the states in addition to a special majority.

practiceMcqs.question: Who was the legendary constitutional lawyer who successfully argued the Kesavananda Bharati case defending the Basic Structure Doctrine?
practiceMcqs.explanation: Nani Palkhivala argued the historic Kesavananda Bharati case (1973), establishing the Basic Structure Doctrine to prevent parliamentary overreach.
